page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 62 GB of RAM, equating to 7.75 GB of memory per core. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with an x86_64 architecture, this instance utilizes 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled on a dedicated CPU allocation. The processor operates at a base speed of 2.3 GHz and features a substantial 260 MB L3 cache, alongside 2 MB L2 and 48 KB L1d caches. It does not include bundled local storage or GPUs, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results show strong single-threaded performance, with Geekbench single-core scores of 2270.0 and single-threaded gzip compression of 34.83 GB/sec placing in the top tier. Similarly, single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 14.96 GB/sec and Geekbench text processing at 3059.0 rank in the top 10%. Multi-core and memory performance generally align with the middle tier, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 8519.0, a Passmark CPU score of 12270.59, and a Redis SET throughput of 12.37 million operations per second. Large-block memory bandwidth reaches 61.4 GB/sec for read operations and 35.37 GB/sec for write operations.

The instance exhibits performance trade-offs, particularly in large-scale language model workloads. While prompt processing for a small 135M model achieves an average 840.63 tokens per second, text generation for a medium 7B model drops to a weak 7.05 tokens per second, and prompt processing for a large 70B model is limited to 5.68 tokens per second. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and strong single-core capabilities, the c4-highmem-8 is well-suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, text processing, and lightweight serialization tasks, rather than heavy LLM text generation or storage-reliant workloads.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-8 is an x86_64 virtual machine configured with 8 vCPUs and 62 GB of RAM, delivering 7.75 GB of memory per core. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz with 4 physical cores, hyper-threading, and a 260 MB L3 cache. This instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core capabilities, including a Geekbench single-core score of 2270.0 and single-threaded gzip compression of 34.83 GB/sec. Conversely, multi-core operations and memory bandwidth sit in the average tier, and LLM text generation for medium-to-large models is weak. The c4-highmem-8 is highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and text processing tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-8 is an x86_64 instance with 8 vCPUs, 62 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C CPU. It delivers top-tier single-core performance and is optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-8 features 8 vCPUs, 62 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, delivering top-tier single-core performance.
tagline: An x86_64 virtual machine featuring high memory density, strong single-core performance, and advanced Intel Xeon processing for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 8 vCPUs and 62 GB RAM providing 7.75 GB per core
Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 260 MB L3 cache
Top-tier single-core and text processing benchmark performance
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for memory-intensive databases and text processing
